name: deskport
description: Sync an SSH-remote workspace to your local machine and launch configured dev targets — for Electron and other GUI apps that must run where the display is.

deskport.clonePath.markdownDescription:
Base directory on the **local machine** where remote workspaces are mirrored (cloned). Each launch lands under `<clonePath>/<ssh-host>/<absolute-remote-path>/`, so the same remote folder always maps to the same local path regardless of which parent directory you opened in VSCode.

Leave empty to use the default `~/.deskport-mirrors`. A leading `~` expands to your home directory. Because this is a local filesystem path, it can be set globally in **User** settings or overridden per project in **Workspace** settings.

deskport.workspaceMaxHeight.markdownDescription:
How many directory levels DeskPort walks **up** from a target's package, looking for a `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, before launching.

When one is found within this many levels, that directory is treated as the workspace root: DeskPort mirrors the root manifests (`pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, root `package.json`, `.npmrc`, `.pnpmfile.cjs`), the target package, and the target's `workspace:*` dependency packages (resolved transitively), then runs `install` from that root so pnpm linkage is established correctly. When none is found within the cap, DeskPort falls back to syncing just the target's own folder.

When installing at a discovered root, a bare `pnpm install` / `pnpm i` in the package's `install` is automatically scoped to the launched package and its dependencies (`pnpm install --filter "<name>..."`); any other `install` command runs verbatim. DeskPort never substitutes a different package manager — the command always comes from your `.deskport/config.json`.

Set to `0` to disable the walk-up entirely. A per-package `"workspaceMaxHeight"` in `.deskport/config.json` overrides this setting.

scripts.package: pnpm run compile && vsce package --no-dependencies -o deskport.vsix
